selectors.role: PRODUCTION DEFAULT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION unset or =v4, and any unknown value)

entrypoint.delegates_to: scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query (line 9210; a ~13k-line orchestrator inside a 22,303-line module) which runs the full pipeline-A (retrieval -> evidence selection -> multi-section generation -> strict_verify -> evaluator gate -> report assembly)
entrypoint.signature: async build_and_run_v4(vector_id: str, query: str, application: str='', region: str='', stage: int=1, max_iterations: int=3, max_execution_minutes: int=60, resume: bool=False, enable_dashboard: bool=True, document_ids: list[str]|None=None, steer_callback: Callable|None=None, research_brief: str='') -> dict
entrypoint.run_one_query_signature: async run_one_query(q: dict, out_root: Path, *, four_role_transport=None, four_role_inputs=None, four_role_input_builder=None, query_index=None, query_total=None, resume=False) -> dict

selectors.fixed_input: Reuse the acceptance THIN research question verbatim: 'What is the HbA1c-lowering efficacy of tirzepatide 15mg AND what is known about its long-term cardiovascular safety (major adverse cardiovascular events, SURMOUNT-MMO or equivalent outcome trial data)?'. Wrap as build_and_run_v4(vector_id='replay_v4', query=<THIN>, application='clinical', ...). Internally synthesized into q={slug,domain='clinical',question,amplified:[]}. Small budgets (max_iterations=1, max_execution_minutes low) keep the record cheap but a full-graph run is still much larger than the outline-only oracle.
nondeterminism_sources: LLM: ALL model crossings go through OpenRouterClient.generate / generate_structured (37 files call it) -> ALREADY freezable by tests/oracle/llm_cassette.py, BUT this path issues FAR more (and concurrent/threaded) LLM calls than the sequential outline loop the cassette's ordinal call_id assumes -- concurrent identical requests would collide on the ordinal id (documented cassette limitation) and need a context-derived call_id

Retrieval + BROWSER: run_live_retrieval is the single seam (Playwright/Crawl4AI fetch, Serper, and the S2/OpenAlex/NCBI enrichment endpoints all live INSIDE it) -> ALREADY freezable by tests/oracle/retrieval_cassette.py; this also neutralizes the broken browser

content_relevance_judge (retrieval-side, W2, DEFAULT-ON via PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=1): a LOCAL GPU CrossEncoder (Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B, cuda/cpu fallback) scores every passage -> a NEW non-determinism source NOT crossed by either existing cassette (device-dependent float scores); would need PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 or a reranker_predict_fn freeze. Its GLM escalation is a raw path too

generator-side relevance_judge: raw httpx.Client POSTing directly to {OPENROUTER_BASE_URL}/chat/completions -- BYPASSES OpenRouterClient, so llm_cassette does NOT catch it. DEFAULT-OFF (PG_RELEVANCE_GATE) so off the default path, but a latent extra seam

Timestamps: run_id = f'SWEEP_{domain}_{slug}_{int(time.time())}' (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9291); datetime.now(utc).isoformat() baked into UI JSON 'timestamps.completed' and started_at; many manifest fields

RNG/hash: content_deduplicator MinHash uses builtin hash() (PYTHONHASHSEED-randomized across processes; NOT pinned by any harness); scattered random.seed(42) only in specific spots; dict/set iteration order in evidence selection
Wall-clock elapsed baked into manifest cost/telemetry and (as in the outline oracle) potentially into LLM-prompt-visible strings -> would need frozen-clock carve-outs
Per-run cost ledger + judge telemetry counters (reset per run but value-dependent on the above)
selectors.feasibility_estimate: LOW / HARD in this env. Feasibility is BLOCKED for a fresh record here: (a) no API keys in process env or repo .env (keys exist only at /workspace/POLARIS/.env; a record still spends real money and is much costlier than the outline oracle), and (b) the browser is CONFIRMED broken (Playwright TargetClosedError) so a live record of run_live_retrieval fetches cannot run here anyway. Even with keys+browser elsewhere, byte-identical replay is substantially harder than the outline oracle (which needed ~4 non-determinism fixes / 21 carve-outs in acceptance_portable.py): the full graph adds (1) a local GPU reranker (device-dependent, uncovered seam), (2) a raw-httpx relevance seam bypassing llm_cassette, (3) concurrent/threaded LLM calls that break the cassette's sequential ordinal call_id, (4) far more timestamp/RNG/hash surfaces. Realistic path: freeze both existing seams + disable PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE (or inject a deterministic reranker_predict_fn) + keep PG_RELEVANCE_GATE off + add a context-derived call_id for concurrency + frozen-clock/PYTHONHASHSEED=0 carve-outs, recorded ON A HOST WITH KEYS+WORKING BROWSER, then replayed here browser-free.

selectors.role: DORMANT (only when PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v3; never set at runtime anywhere)

entrypoint.signature: async build_and_run_v3(vector_id, query, application='', region='', stage=1, max_iterations=3, max_execution_minutes=60, resume=False, enable_dashboard=True, document_ids=None, steer_callback=None, research_brief='') -> dict (identical UI signature to v1)
entrypoint.internals: instantiates OpenRouterClient() directly, builds a LangGraph (build_v3_graph) with a side-channel evidence_store

selectors.fixed_input: Same THIN question as v4, via build_and_run_v3(vector_id='replay_v3', query=<THIN>, application='clinical', max_iterations=1, ...).
nondeterminism_sources: LLM via OpenRouterClient (freezable by llm_cassette; 9 nondeterminism markers in graph_v3.py)
Retrieval/browser via run_live_retrieval (freezable by retrieval_cassette; broken browser neutralized on replay)
content_relevance_judge local GPU reranker if reached (default-ON) -- same uncovered seam as v4

datetime.now(utc).isoformat() started_at; os.environ mutation PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS; time-based budget gating (PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS controls loop termination -> wall-clock can change control flow/number of LLM calls -> replay drift)
PYTHONHASHSEED-sensitive set/dict ordering
selectors.feasibility_estimate: LOW-to-MEDIUM, and LOWER PRIORITY. Same env blockers (no keys in-process, broken browser -> cannot record here). It is a DORMANT selector (never used at runtime), so a full byte-identical replay fixture is of limited value vs. cost; the v3 LangGraph is smaller/more sequential than v4 (better fit for the cassette's ordinal call_id) but its time-budget-driven loop termination is a real replay-drift risk that needs a frozen clock. If any dormant selector is fixtured it should be de-scoped to a coarse 'runs to completion + status' smoke rather than full byte-identity.

Dormant selector; the v2 CRAG loop has the most nondeterminism markers of the legacy three and writes raw time.monotonic elapsed into its result, so full byte-identity needs the same frozen-clock treatment as the outline oracle plus both cassette seams. Recommend coarse smoke-fixture, not byte-identity.

entrypoint.signature: async build_and_run(vector_id, query, application, region, stage=1, max_iterations=3, max_execution_minutes=int(os.getenv('PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES','60')), resume=False, enable_dashboard=True, document_ids=None, steer_callback=None, research_brief=None) -> ResearchState (NOTE: application+region are REQUIRED positional here, unlike v2/v3/v4 which default them to '')
entrypoint.internals: create_initial_state + full LangGraph astream(); SQLite checkpointer for crash-recovery/resume (AREA-8); Rich dashboard (AREA-7); budget guard PG_BUDGET_GUARD_USD default 150

selectors.fixed_input: Same THIN question BUT application and region must be passed explicitly (they are required positional): build_and_run(vector_id='replay_v1', query=<THIN>, application='clinical', region='global', max_iterations=1, ...).
nondeterminism_sources: LLM via OpenRouterClient (freezable; 10 nondeterminism markers)
Retrieval/browser via run_live_retrieval (freezable; broken browser neutralized)
content_relevance_judge local GPU reranker if reached (default-ON, uncovered)

SQLite checkpointer writes (AREA-8): timestamps + a stateful DB file on disk -> a NEW persistence non-determinism source not present in v2/v3/v4; would need a fresh/isolated checkpoint dir and likely resume=False + deterministic path
astream() streaming + Rich dashboard (enable_dashboard=True default) -> set enable_dashboard=False for a deterministic run
datetime/time timestamps; budget-guard wall-clock gating; PYTHONHASHSEED-sensitive ordering
graph.py is the largest module (99k bytes) -> broadest surface
selectors.feasibility_estimate: LOWEST, LOWER PRIORITY. Same env blockers PLUS a SQLite checkpointer that persists timestamped state to disk (an extra non-determinism source none of the other selectors have) and the largest code surface. Dormant selector. Recommend coarse smoke-fixture at most; full byte-identity is the hardest of the four.

result.baseline:
BASELINE (the 'before' reference state, collected in worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase0a5 on branch chore/review-readiness-0a5, forked from chore/review-readiness-phase0 @ 7adea84).

WHAT EXISTS AS A TRUSTWORTHY 'BEFORE' TODAY (Oracle Layer 2, outline-agent path only):
- tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_golden.json  sha256=9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 (3184 bytes) -- the pinned byte-identical golden for the OUTLINE-AGENT path (THIN + SATURATED scenarios), replayed browser-free/network-free via llm_cassette + retrieval_cassette + _frozen_step_clock. This is the ONLY currently-pinned deterministic 'before' in the repo and it does NOT cover any full-graph selector.
- Cassette tapes: acceptance_llm.jsonl (243,281 B) + acceptance_retrieval.jsonl (397,150 B).
- Result artifacts: acceptance_result.json (3210 B), acceptance_result_thin.json (1280 B), acceptance_result_saturated.json (598 B).
- SECRET-SAFE anchor manifest: docs/review_readiness/baseline_manifest.json (anchor b6e8ef5; model routing = z-ai/glm-5.2 via /workspace/POLARIS/.env override of the deepseek default; secrets recorded as sha256 digests only; no global RNG seed; PYTHONHASHSEED NOT pinned).

ENVIRONMENT FACTS CONFIRMED THIS SESSION (the feasibility constraints):
- Browser BROKEN: Playwright chromium launch fails with TargetClosedError ('Target page, context or browser has been closed') -- a fresh LIVE record of any run_live_retrieval fetch CANNOT run in this env. Replay is browser-free, so this only blocks recording, not replaying.
- API keys: NONE in process env; NO repo-local .env. Keys exist ONLY at /workspace/POLARIS/.env (OPENROUTER/SERPER/ZYTE + 17 others present). A full-graph record spends real money and is far costlier than the outline oracle.
- All four entrypoints IMPORT CLEANLY under /opt/conda/bin/python (CPython 3.11): build_and_run_v4, run_one_query, build_and_run_v3, build_and_run (v2), build_and_run (v1). The repo's declared runtime is 3.11.10.

NO full-graph replay fixture ('before') was CREATED this session -- the two hard env blockers (no in-process keys + broken browser) make a fresh, faithful LIVE record impossible here, and per the investigate-then-consult rule I did not fabricate one. The DELIVERABLE of 0A-5 is this characterization + the existing outline-golden as the proven Layer-2 baseline the same machinery would extend from.

RECOMMENDED PATH TO A REAL v4 'BEFORE' (must run on a host WITH keys AND a working browser, then replay here):
1) Freeze BOTH existing seams (llm_cassette on OpenRouterClient.generate/_structured; retrieval_cassette on run_live_retrieval -- this also freezes the browser + Serper + S2/OpenAlex/NCBI).
2) Neutralize the two UNCOVERED seams: set PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 (or inject a deterministic reranker_predict_fn) to remove the local GPU reranker; keep PG_RELEVANCE_GATE off (default) to avoid the raw-httpx OpenRouter bypass.
3) Add a context-derived call_id to llm_cassette (the current ordinal id assumes a SEQUENTIAL loop; the full graph issues concurrent/threaded generate calls).
4) Frozen-clock carve-outs (as the outline oracle did) + PYTHONHASHSEED=0 + normalize run_id/timestamps out of the canonical artifact.
5) v1 additionally needs an isolated SQLite checkpoint dir + enable_dashboard=False.
Only v4 is the production default and worth the full byte-identity investment; v1/v2/v3 are DORMANT (never selected at runtime) and should get at most a coarse 'runs-to-status' smoke fixture, not byte-identity.

blocked_selectors.primary_blocker: BROWSER WALL (hard, confirmed): a fresh record of run_live_retrieval requires a live page fetch, but headless chromium cannot launch in this env — chrome-headless-shell exits 127 with 'libnspr4.so: cannot open shared object file'. The OS shared lib is absent from the whole system (find / -name libnspr4.so* => empty; ldconfig has no nspr). This is the same fault surfacing as the pre-existing tests/test_clean_state.py collection error (playwright TargetClosedError). Without a live fetch there is no way to RECORD the retrieval cassette here, and the task forbids faking a fixture.
secondary_blockers: UNCOVERED SEAM — content_relevance_judge local GPU reranker: PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE defaults ON (content_relevance_judge.py:98; winner_firing_gate.py:91), routing every passage through a CrossEncoder (Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B). torch reports CUDA available in /opt/conda python, so scores are device-dependent floats crossed by NEITHER existing cassette. Would need PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 or an injected deterministic reranker_predict_fn.

CONCURRENCY — the llm/retrieval cassettes use an ORDINAL call_id per identical request, correct only for a SEQUENTIAL loop. run_honest_sweep_r3.py has 18 asyncio.gather/executor/create_task sites; concurrent identical generate() calls would collide on the ordinal id and misreplay. Needs a context-derived call_id before the full-graph path is replayable.

RAW-HTTPX RELEVANCE SEAM (latent): generator/relevance_judge.py POSTs directly to {OPENROUTER_BASE_URL}/chat/completions via httpx (relevance_judge.py:291-314), bypassing OpenRouterClient so llm_cassette does not catch it. It is DEFAULT-OFF (PG_RELEVANCE_GATE default '0', relevance_judge.py:77/178) so off the v4 default path, but a real uncovered seam if ever enabled.

TIMESTAMP/RNG/HASH surfaces beyond the outline oracle: run_id=SWEEP_{...}_{int(time.time())} (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9291), datetime.now(utc) in UI JSON, content_deduplicator MinHash uses builtin hash() (PYTHONHASHSEED-randomized, pinned by no harness), plus wall-clock baked into cost/telemetry — far larger normalization surface than the ~4 fixes/21 carve-outs the outline oracle needed.
blocked_selectors.note_on_keys: Correction to the prior writeup: OPENROUTER_API_KEY and SERPER_API_KEY ARE reachable — they are present in /workspace/POLARIS/.env, which acceptance_portable's dotenv resolution loads (repo .env is absent, so /workspace/POLARIS/.env wins). So a record is not key-blocked; it is BROWSER-blocked (and would spend real money on a full-graph run). Recording must happen on a host with working OS chromium libs, then replay browser-free here.

blocked_selectors.status: BLOCKED — deferred to 3C (recommend coarse smoke, not byte-identity)
blocked_selectors.primary_blocker: Same absolute browser wall (libnspr4.so missing) — cannot record run_live_retrieval live here.
secondary_blockers: Same default-ON GPU reranker uncovered seam.
Time-budget-driven loop termination: PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS gates the loop, so wall-clock can change the NUMBER of LLM calls -> replay drift; needs a frozen clock.
Dormant selector: full byte-identity is low value vs cost.

blocked_selectors.role: DORMANT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v2 / legacy PG_V2_ENABLED=1; never set at runtime)
blocked_selectors.status: BLOCKED — deferred to 3C (recommend coarse smoke, not byte-identity)
secondary_blockers: Same default-ON GPU reranker uncovered seam.
Highest nondeterminism-marker count of the legacy three (14); writes raw time.monotonic() elapsed into its result -> needs the same frozen-clock treatment as the outline oracle.

blocked_selectors.role: DORMANT (PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v1; never set at runtime; original entrypoint)
blocked_selectors.status: BLOCKED — deferred to 3C (recommend coarse smoke at most; hardest of the four)
secondary_blockers: Same default-ON GPU reranker uncovered seam.

SQLite checkpointer (AREA-8) persists timestamped state to disk — an EXTRA persistence non-determinism source none of the other selectors have; needs an isolated/fresh checkpoint dir + resume=False + deterministic path.
astream() streaming + Rich dashboard (enable_dashboard=True default) — must set enable_dashboard=False.
Largest code surface (graph.py ~99k) + required positional application/region args (unlike v2/v3/v4).

result.collection_after: 16729 tests collected, 11 pre-existing collection errors (UNCHANGED from baseline). No fixture files were written, so collection is byte-for-byte the baseline. The 11 errors are pre-existing and unrelated to graph fixtures: tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py, tests/polaris_graph/test_demo_smoke.py, tests/polaris_v6/api/test_auth.py, tests/scripts/test_run_paid_evaluator_scoring.py, tests/test_clean_state.py (itself the playwright TargetClosedError browser wall), tests/unit/test_clarification_agent.py, tests/unit/test_hle_benchmark.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_api.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_cli.py, tests/unit/test_mesh_snapshot.py, tests/unit/test_perspective_tracking.py.
result.notes:
HONEST OUTCOME: 0 of 4 selectors pinned; all 4 BLOCKED and deferred-to-3C. No fixtures written (the task explicitly forbids faking one when a selector cannot be pinned).

WHAT I VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY (not taken on faith from the writeup):
1. Interpreter: system python3 lacks dotenv/playwright/torch. The project interpreter is /opt/conda/bin/python — it HAS dotenv, playwright, torch (torch.cuda.is_available()=True), sentence_transformers, httpx. build_and_run_v4 imports cleanly under it. (The prior 'no deps / broken' framing was measured against the wrong interpreter.)
2. All four entrypoints exist: pipeline_a_ui_adapter.py:187 build_and_run_v4; graph_v3.py:711 build_and_run_v3; graph_v2.py:757 build_and_run; graph.py:1288 build_and_run; run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9210 run_one_query.
3. BROWSER WALL (the decisive, hard blocker): playwright IS installed, but launching chromium fails with exit 127 — 'chrome-headless-shell: error while loading shared libraries: libnspr4.so: cannot open shared object file'. libnspr4.so is absent system-wide (find / and ldconfig both empty). The required OS chromium runtime libs are not installed here. => run_live_retrieval CANNOT do a live fetch => the retrieval cassette CANNOT be RECORDED in this env. Replay-only would need a pre-existing tape, and none exists for the graph path (only the outline oracle's acceptance_retrieval.jsonl exists, which is outline-scoped, not full-graph).
4. KEYS: contrary to the prior note, OPENROUTER_API_KEY and SERPER_API_KEY ARE present in /workspace/POLARIS/.env and reachable via the same dotenv resolution acceptance_portable uses (repo .env absent). So the block is NOT keys; it is the browser + uncovered seams. (A record would also spend real money on a full-graph run.)
5. UNCOVERED SEAMS on the v4 default path: PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE defaults ON -> local CUDA CrossEncoder reranker (device-dependent floats, crossed by neither cassette). Generator relevance_judge posts raw httpx to /chat/completions (bypasses OpenRouterClient/llm_cassette) but is default-OFF via PG_RELEVANCE_GATE. 18 asyncio concurrency sites in the orchestrator break the cassette's sequential ordinal call_id.

REALISTIC PATH TO PIN v4 (for 3C, on a host with working OS chromium libs + keys): (a) record ON a host where chromium launches; (b) set PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=0 OR inject a deterministic reranker_predict_fn; (c) keep PG_RELEVANCE_GATE off; (d) add a context-derived call_id to both cassettes for concurrency; (e) add frozen-clock + PYTHONHASHSEED=0 carve-outs and normalize run_id/timestamps/MinHash-hash in the canonical artifact (a MUCH larger surface than the outline oracle's ~4 fixes / 21 carve-outs); then replay browser-free here. v1/v2/v3 are DORMANT (never selected at runtime) and each add their own extra seam (v1 SQLite checkpointer + dashboard; v2 raw time.monotonic in result; v3 PG_V3_TOTAL_BUDGET_SECONDS wall-clock-driven loop termination) — recommend a coarse 'runs-to-completion + status' smoke fixture rather than full byte-identity, and only after v4 is pinned.

COLLECTION: unchanged at baseline (16729 collected / 11 pre-existing errors). No files added.

codex_points: Codex verdict: SUFFICIENT for THIS readiness review; no additional selector fixture is required now.
Rationale: 3C is owner-deferred and no selector behavior is being deleted or changed in this review, so a readiness gate does not require proving a future deletion.
The four blocked fixtures (v4 full-graph + dormant v1/v2/v3) may legitimately ride with the deferred 3C effort.
CONDITION carried forward: 3C must remain GATED until its agreed replay/smoke coverage exists (v4 full byte-identity; coarse runs-to-status smoke acceptable for the dormant v1/v2/v3).
IMPORTANT caveat: the existing outline-agent oracle (acceptance_golden.json) does NOT count as full-v4 coverage; it only pins the outline-agent segment of the v4 path, not the full graph.

The block is a hard environmental wall (missing OS lib libnspr4.so -> chromium cannot launch, cannot RECORD the retrieval cassette), not unwillingness; recording must happen on a host with working chromium libs + keys, then replay browser-free.
No fixtures were fabricated and test collection is byte-for-byte the baseline (16729 collected / 11 pre-existing errors unchanged), so nothing in this review regressed.
